questions.question: The Indian government is set to implement the 'One State, One RRB' (Regional Rural Bank) policy to improve operational efficiency, reduce competition and consolidate the existing 43 RRBs into ̲_______.
The Planning Commission used a technique called 'perspective planning' for formulating five year plans in India. Which of the following best describes 'perspective planning'?
The concept of a mixed economy was central to India’s early planning strategies. In the context of India’s Five-Year Plans, what does the term 'mixed economy' refer to?
questions.option_3: Coexistence of public and private sectors
questions.option_4: Balanced growth of rural and urban areas

questions.question: In February 2025, which bank of India has announced $10 billion three-year dollar/rupee swap auction?
What was the size of the special economic and comprehensive package launched by the Central Government to fight COVID-19 pandemic in India?
Labour reforms have remained a key agenda in the post-1991 economic policy landscape. How many labour codes were consolidated by the Indian government under the labour reforms enacted in 2019-2020?
The Planning Commission was established to steer India's five year plans. Who was the first chairman of the Planning Commission of India?
What was the trend in the unemployment rate in usual status for persons of age 15 years and above, for urban areas, from FY23 to FY24, as per the Department of Economic Affairs report?
The PM-KISAN scheme launched by the Central Government falls under which category of boxes under the WTO’s Agreement on Agriculture?
The Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Limited (DFCCIL) was established as a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) under which of the following Acts?
In March 2025, India introduced anti-dumping duties on five products imported from which country?
The 1991 reforms led to major tax policy changes to boost efficiency and compliance. Which tax reform, implemented in 2017, aimed to unify India’s indirect tax structure?
The government of India decided that Medium-Term and Long-Term Government Deposit (MLTGD) components of Gold Monetisation Scheme (GMS) shall be discontinued from:
Liberalisation enabled more autonomy and competition in the banking sector. Which of the following was the first private bank to be set up in India after the 1991 reforms?
What was the theme of Financial Literacy Week 2025 observed from 24 to 28 February?
questions.option_1: Financial Literacy: A Step Towards Empowerment
questions.option_4: Credit Discipline and Credit from Formal Institutions

questions.question: The 1991 economic reforms in India led to rapid industrialisation and expansion of energy-intensive sectors. Which market-based mechanism has India adopted to balance industrial growth, energy efficiency and emissions?
Per capita income is an indicator of economic development. What happened to India’s per capita income post 1991 economic reforms?
According to the Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) report, what was the Labour Force Participation Rate (LFPR) for women in 2021-22?
Which state recorded the lowest unemployment rate according to the PLFS 2022-23?
Which sector is the largest recipient of foreign direct investment in the first half of financial year 2024-25?

Financial inclusion has been a major goal of post-1991 economic reforms in India. Which of the following flagship schemes was launched in 2014 to promote financial inclusion through universal access to banking facilities?
Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS) is an electronic platform for facilitating the financing/discounting of trade receivables of which sector?
According to the IMF's April 2025 Global Financial Stability Report, how much did global stock returns decline on average during major geopolitical risk events?
Fiscal consolidation has remained a target of macroeconomic reforms. What was the targeted fiscal deficit as a percentage of GDP under the original FRBM Act 2003 ?
India’s dependency on food aid reduced after the Green Revolution. Which US program had earlier provided wheat aid to India?
